results.content:
ArchiMind ends that.

ArchiMind is a native Revit plugin that turns California building code into a live, intelligent layer inside your model. The moment you run an analysis, every room, door, stair, corridor, and restroom is evaluated against CBC 2025 — automatically, instantly, and without leaving Revit. No exports. No spreadsheets. No guesswork.

Built for the Code That Actually Governs California Projects

California doesn't run on IBC.

ArchiMind was built from the ground up for this stack: [...] aec+tech
Advanced Search

ArchiMind

# ArchiMind

Website for ArchiMind

ARCHIMIND is a native Autodesk Revit add-in that runs California Building Code (CBC 2025 & 2022) compliance checks directly inside Revit. It automates 182+ code checks across six chapters — including egress, accessibility, plumbing, height and area, fire resistance, and light and ventilation — and delivers results in under 60 seconds. Designed for architecture firms, ARCHIMIND eliminates manual code lookups, reduces permit rejection risk, and integrates seamlessly into the existing Revit workflow without switching tools or exporting files.

Building Information Modeling (BIM)

Artificial Intelligence (AI)

Architectural Design

Building Performance

Key Facts

Similar Tools

FAQ

Comments

## Gallery [...] Occupancy Load · Egress · Stairs & Ramps · Fire Resistance · Height & Area · Light & Ventilation · Chapter 11B Accessibility · Restroom Fixtures · Plumbing Fixture Count · EVCS Parking · Exit Signs · DSA Path of Travel

Every result is tied to a specific Revit element and cited to the exact code section. Not "something might be wrong." Exactly what, exactly where, exactly why.

Not Just Flagging — Fixing

Most compliance tools stop at flagging. ArchiMind goes further.

Click GoTo on any failing check — Revit zooms directly to the element. Click Fix — ArchiMind corrects it automatically. Door too narrow? Fixed. Handrail too low? Fixed. Ramp too steep? Fixed. Stair too narrow? Fixed. Corridor too tight? Fixed. Every fix is fully undoable with Ctrl+Z. Re-run the analysis to confirm.

results.title: GitHub Repository Analysis with Full-Context AI
results.content:
# GitHub Repository Analysis with Full-Context AI

RepoMind helps teams evaluate unfamiliar repositories by mapping architecture, tracing implementation behavior, and surfacing risk hotspots that affect delivery confidence.

The workflow is designed for practical decisions: whether to adopt a dependency, how to onboard quickly, and where to focus review effort when timelines are tight.

## What this workflow delivers

RepoMind does more than summarize files. It highlights how modules connect, where critical logic resides, and which areas deserve deeper review before engineering investment increases.

### Architecture clarity for faster onboarding

Teams can understand major boundaries and dependencies early, reducing time spent in manual code discovery. [...] ## Frequently Asked Questions

### What is GitHub repository analysis in RepoMind?

It is a workflow that maps architecture, behavior paths, and risk hotspots so teams can make faster technical decisions.

### When should teams run repository analysis?

Run it during onboarding, due diligence, migration planning, security triage preparation, and major release checkpoints.

### Can this be used for open-source adoption decisions?

Yes. Teams use it to evaluate maintainability, architecture complexity, and likely remediation effort before adoption.

### Who benefits the most from this workflow?

Platform teams, senior engineers, technical leads, and security reviewers who need fast context on unfamiliar codebases.

### Does it replace code review or security scanning? [...] ### Does it replace code review or security scanning?

No. It complements both by establishing a shared architecture context before deeper review and security workflows.

### What should happen after the first analysis run?

Use identified hotspots to prioritize review and security actions, then track improvements through recurring analysis checkpoints.

## Take the next step

Analyze one critical repository and convert context into a prioritized review and security plan.
